title: Ou Chuliang

infobox.value: |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Men's [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") | | | | Representing  [China](/wiki/China "China") | | | | [AFC Asian Cup](/wiki/AFC_Asian_Cup "AFC Asian Cup") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [1992 Japan](/wiki/1992_AFC_Asian_Cup "1992 AFC Asian Cup") | [Team](/wiki/1992_AFC_Asian_Cup_squads#China "1992 AFC Asian Cup squads") | | [Asian Games](/wiki/Asian_Games "Asian Games") | | | | Silver medal – second place | [1994 Hiroshima](/wiki/1994_Asian_Games "1994 Asian Games") | [Football](/wiki/Football_at_the_1994_Asian_Games "Football at the 1994 Asian Games") | | | |

([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 区楚良; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 區楚良; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Oū Chǔliáng*; [Jyutping](/wiki/Jyutping "Jyutping"): *Au1 Cho2 Leung4*; born 26 August 1968 in [Guangzhou](/wiki/Guangzhou "Guangzhou"), [Guangdong](/wiki/Guangdong "Guangdong")) is an assistant coach and former [Chinese](/wiki/China "China") international [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper_(association_football) "Goalkeeper (association football)"). As a player, he was goalkeeper for [Guangdong Hongyuan](/wiki/Guangdong_Hongyuan_F.C. "Guangdong Hongyuan F.C."), [Shanghai Shenhua](/wiki/Shanghai_Shenhua "Shanghai Shenhua"), [Yunnan Hongta](/wiki/Yunnan_Hongta "Yunnan Hongta") and [Chongqing Qiche](/wiki/Chongqing_Qiche "Chongqing Qiche") while internationally he was a participant of the [Chinese football team](/wiki/China_national_football_team "China national football team") that took part in the [2002 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2002_FIFA_World_Cup "2002 FIFA World Cup").

["China 1993"](https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/china93.html). [Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation](/wiki/Rec.Sport.Soccer_Statistics_Foundation "Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation"). 6 November 2009. Retrieved 8 August 2012.

["China League 1997"](https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/china97.html). [Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation](/wiki/Rec.Sport.Soccer_Statistics_Foundation "Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation"). 21 June 2003. Retrieved 8 August 2012.

["Asian Cup 1996 VA Emirate .:. Gruppe C"](http://www.weltfussball.de/spielbericht/asian-cup-1996-in-den-va-emiraten-gruppe-c-china-usbekistan/). weltfussball.de. 6 December 1996. Retrieved 8 August 2012.

["十九路军淞沪抗日七十八师师长区寿年"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160308112026/http://www.ycwb.com/gb/content/2005-07/28/content_949316.htm). Yangcheng Evening News. 28 July 2005. Archived from [the original](http://www.ycwb.com/gb/content/2005-07/28/content_949316.htm) on 8 March 2016. Retrieved 8 June 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Chinese footballer and coach

In this [Chinese name](/wiki/Chinese_name "Chinese name"), the [family name](/wiki/Chinese_surname "Chinese surname") is  *[Ou (surname)](/wiki/Ou_(surname) "Ou (surname)")* and  *Chu* is a [generation name](/wiki/Generation_name "Generation name").

Ou Chuliang   
 区楚良

**Ou Chuliang** ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 区楚良; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 區楚良; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Oū Chǔliáng*; [Jyutping](/wiki/Jyutping "Jyutping"): *Au1 Cho2 Leung4*; born 26 August 1968 in [Guangzhou](/wiki/Guangzhou "Guangzhou"), [Guangdong](/wiki/Guangdong "Guangdong")) is an assistant coach and former [Chinese](/wiki/China "China") international [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper_(association_football) "Goalkeeper (association football)"). As a player, he was goalkeeper for [Guangdong Hongyuan](/wiki/Guangdong_Hongyuan_F.C. "Guangdong Hongyuan F.C."), [Shanghai Shenhua](/wiki/Shanghai_Shenhua "Shanghai Shenhua"), [Yunnan Hongta](/wiki/Yunnan_Hongta "Yunnan Hongta") and [Chongqing Qiche](/wiki/Chongqing_Qiche "Chongqing Qiche") while internationally he was a participant of the [Chinese football team](/wiki/China_national_football_team "China national football team") that took part in the [2002 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2002_FIFA_World_Cup "2002 FIFA World Cup").

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ou_Chuliang&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

Ou started his football career with [Guangdong Hongyuan F.C.](/wiki/Guangdong_Hongyuan_F.C. "Guangdong Hongyuan F.C.") during the 1988 football league season. With them Ou would see Guangdong establish themselves as a professional football club in the Chinese league system and also help them come second in the 1993 league season.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Guangdong, however were relegated in the 1997 league season and Ou Chuliang was transferred to [Shanghai Shenhua](/wiki/Shanghai_Shenhua "Shanghai Shenhua") at the beginning of the 1998 league season.[[2]](#cite_note-2) With Shanghai Ou Chuliang would make an immediate impact by helping them come second within the league as well as aiding them in their [Chinese FA Cup](/wiki/Chinese_FA_Cup "Chinese FA Cup") win. [Yunnan Hongta](/wiki/Yunnan_Hongta "Yunnan Hongta") who were a newly promoted football team wanted to take Ou Chuliang in their attempt to establish themselves within the top tier and Ou would help them do this by quickly establish himself in the team by playing in 26 league games and aiding Yunnan Hongta avoid relegation.[[3]](#cite_note-Oū,_Chǔliáng-3) Qu would spend several more seasons with Yunnan before being transferred to [Chongqing Lifan F.C.](/wiki/Chongqing_Lifan_F.C. "Chongqing Lifan F.C.") in 2004 eventually ending his career with them.

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ou_Chuliang&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International career")]

Ou made his international debut in a friendly match against [Canada](/wiki/Canada_men%27s_national_soccer_team "Canada men's national soccer team") on 2 April 1992 in a 5–2 defeat.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Despite the defeat, Ou would be given the chance to play in several other friendlies which were enough to be called up to the Chinese squad that took part in the [1992 AFC Asian Cup](/wiki/1992_AFC_Asian_Cup "1992 AFC Asian Cup") where he played understudy to [Fu Yubin](/wiki/Fu_Yubin "Fu Yubin"). Ou would rise to prominence when he replaced Fu Yubin as the first choice goalkeeper for China in the [1996 AFC Asian Cup](/wiki/1996_AFC_Asian_Cup "1996 AFC Asian Cup") by starting the first game of the tournament against [Uzbekistan](/wiki/Uzbekistan_national_football_team "Uzbekistan national football team").[[5]](#cite_note-5) After a disappointing [1998 World Cup qualifying](/wiki/1998_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(AFC) "1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)") performance, Ou found himself increasingly replaced by [Jiang Jin](/wiki/Jiang_Jin "Jiang Jin") leading up to the [2000 AFC Asian Cup](/wiki/2000_AFC_Asian_Cup "2000 AFC Asian Cup"). Jiang eventually cemented his position at the [2002 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2002_FIFA_World_Cup "2002 FIFA World Cup").

## Management career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ou_Chuliang&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Management career")]

After he retired he would move to the United Kingdom and Germany to study for and achieve a [FIFA](/wiki/FIFA "FIFA") coaching licence. When he returned in 2005 he was offered an assistant goalkeeping position for the [Chinese U-23](/wiki/China_national_under-23_football_team "China national under-23 football team") football team to help them prepare for the [Football at the 2008 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Football_at_the_2008_Summer_Olympics "Football at the 2008 Summer Olympics"). Ou would only stay for a short period before he returned to Shanghai Shenhua to become their goalkeeping coach. By 2008 he was offered another assistant position, this time with the Chinese senior team as their new goalkeeping coach.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ou_Chuliang&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Ou Chuliang's grandfather Ou Shounian ([Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 区寿年) was a general of the [National Revolutionary Army](/wiki/National_Revolutionary_Army "National Revolutionary Army") of the [Republic of China](/wiki/Republic_of_China_(1912%E2%80%931949) "Republic of China (1912–1949)").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ou_Chuliang&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Honours")]

### Player

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ou_Chuliang&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Player")]

**[Shanghai Shenhua](/wiki/Shanghai_Shenhua "Shanghai Shenhua")**[[3]](#cite_note-Oū,_Chǔliáng-3)

* [Chinese FA Cup](/wiki/Chinese_FA_Cup "Chinese FA Cup"): 1998
